elements.sent-2411-007: We construct the first explicit two-sided vertex expanders that bypass the spectral barrier.
elements.sent-2411-008: Previously, the strongest known explicit vertex expanders were given by $d$-regular Ramanujan graphs, whose spectral properties imply that every small subset of vertices $S$ has at least $0.5d|S|$ distinct neighbors.
elements.sent-2411-009: However, it is possible to construct Ramanujan graphs containing a small set $S$ with no more than $0.5d|S|$ neighbors.
elements.sent-2411-010: In fact, no explicit construction was known to break the $0.5 d$-barrier.
elements.sent-2411-011: In this work, we give an explicit construction of an infinite family of $d$-regular graphs (for large enough $d$) where every small set expands by a factor of $\approx 0.6d$.
elements.sent-2411-012: More generally, for large enough $d_1,d_2$, we give an infinite family of $(d_1,d_2)$-biregular graphs where small sets on the left expand by a factor of $\approx 0.6d_1$, and small sets on the right expand by a factor of $\approx 0.6d_2$.
elements.sent-2411-013: In fact, our construction satisfies an even stronger property: small sets on the left and right have unique-neighbor expansion $0.6d_1$ and $0.6d_2$ respectively.
elements.sent-2411-014: Our construction follows the tripartite line product framework of~\cite{HMMP24 , and instantiates it using the face-vertex incidence of the $4$-dimensional Ramanujan clique complex as its base component.}
elements.sent-2411-015: As a key part of our analysis, we derive new bounds on the triangle density of small sets in the Ramanujan clique complex.
elements.sent-2411-016: In this work, we study the problem of constructing explicit vertex expanders.
elements.sent-2411-017: Vertex expansion refers to the property that every ``small enough'' set of vertices should have ``many'' distinct neighbors.
elements.sent-2411-018: Henceforth, we restrict our attention to bipartite graphs.
elements.sent-2411-019: For $d_L > d_R$, we say that a $(d_L, d_R)$-biregular graph $G$ on $(L,R)$ is a $\gamma$-one-sided vertex expander if every subset $S\subseteq L$ of size at most $\eta |L|$ has at least $\gamma d_L |S|$ distinct neighbors in $R$ for some small constant $\eta > 0$.
elements.sent-2411-020: We say that $G$ is a $\gamma$-two-sided vertex expander if, additionally, every small subset $S$ of the right vertices has at least $\gamma d_R |S|$ neighbors.
elements.sent-2411-021: When $G$ achieves the golden standard of $\gamma \approx 1$, in we say that it is a lossless vertex expander.
elements.sent-2411-022: A key motivation to study vertex expanders is for the construction of good error-correcting codes.
elements.sent-2411-023: The seminal work of Sipser and Spielman~\cite{SS96 showed that from any one-sided lossless expander, one can construct a good binary error-correcting code with a linear time decoding algorithm.}
elements.sent-2411-024: In the quantum setting, the work of Lin \& Hsieh~\cite{LH22b showed that two-sided lossless expanders with appropriate algebraic structure can be used to construct good quantum low density parity check codes.}
elements.sent-2411-025: The above applications actually go through a weaker property than lossless expansion.
elements.sent-2411-026: Sufficiently strong vertex expansion implies unique-neighbor expansion, the condition that every small set $S$ has many unique-neighbors, or vertices with exactly one edge to $S$.
elements.sent-2411-027: We say $G$ is a $\gamma$-one-sided unique-neighbor expander if every subset $S$ of $L$ of size at most $\eta|L|$ has at least $\gamma d_L |S|$ unique-neighbors in $R$, and that $G$ is a $\gamma$-two-sided unique neighbor expander if additionally every subset $S$ of $R$ of size at most $\eta|R|$ has at least $\gamma d_R |S|$ unique-neighbors in $L$.
elements.sent-2411-028: Indeed, the above works show that any graph with $>\frac12$-one-sided unique-neighbor expansion yields good classical codes, and $>\frac56$-two-sided unique-neighbor expansion (and an algebraic property) yields good quantum LDPC codes.
elements.sent-2411-029: In this work, we focus on the setting of two-sided vertex expanders, and specifically the task of constructing such objects.
elements.sent-2411-030: Kahale~\cite{Kah95 proved that a Ramanujan graph, i.e., a graph with optimal spectral expansion, is a $\frac{1 {2}$-two-sided vertex expander, and demonstrated a near-Ramanujan graph on which this is tight.}}

elements.subsection-2411-012: Warm up: bounding triangles in small sets in the 3-partite Ramanujan complex
elements.sent-2411-366: Recall that our main goal of this section is to bound the number of triangles contained within small sets of vertices in the $k$-partite Ramanujan complex.
elements.sent-2411-367: As a warm-up, here we will calculate this quantity for the case of $k=3$.
elements.sent-2411-368: The same ideas will generalize to the general case in \Cref{sec:triangles-in-U-general-k , but we find it more transparent to first demonstrate this easier calculation.}
